justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified — explicitly called out 'No request ID / correlation ID in any log line'. (2) API call duration gap identified — 'No duration logged for the SmartyStreets API call'. (3) Metric label gap identified — 'No valid/invalid label on the counter'. (4) Latency histogram absence noted — 'No latency histogram (e.g. validation_request_duration_seconds)'. (5) PII consideration partially addressed — Pippen notes input address is not logged and frames it as a debugging gap, but does NOT explicitly flag PII risk; however, the output does not blindly recommend logging it either, treating it as a gap rather than a mandate. (6) Verdict is NOT READY. (7) Recommendations are specific and actionable throughout (e.g., 'Add correlation ID to all log lines', 'Add valid/invalid/error labels to the request counter', 'Add Prometheus latency histogram').

Recommendations are highly specific and actionable — names exact metrics, labels, and patterns to add

trials.justification: All 7 pass criteria met: (1) Request ID gap identified — 'No request ID / correlation ID in log lines' is the first logging gap. (2) API call duration gap identified — 'No duration logged for the SmartyStreets API call' is the second logging gap. (3) Metric label gap identified — 'validation_requests_total has no label for valid/invalid' called out explicitly with business rationale. (4) Latency histogram absence noted — 'No latency histogram (e.g. validation_request_duration_seconds)' with SLO/p99 context. (5) PII consideration handled appropriately — input address logging is listed as a gap but framed as 'even redacted/hashed', acknowledging the PII dimension rather than blindly recommending raw logging. (6) Verdict is NOT READY. (7) Recommendations are specific and actionable — e.g., 'add request ID to all log lines', 'add result label to counter', 'add validation_request_duration_seconds histogram'.
